expectations.text: Every one of the 8 deals is explicitly categorized as at-risk or stalled using specific criteria from the risk framework — including days since last activity vs. the 14-day and 30-day thresholds, days in stage vs. the provided average stage durations (Discovery 18d, Qualification 14d, Proposal Sent 12d, Negotiation 10d) expressed as a multiple (e.g., 'Helios: 47 days in Discovery = 2.6x the 18-day average'), and whether close date has passed — this level of specificity is what separates actionable deal review from a generic CRM dashboard that any manager could pull themselves
expectations.evidence: All 8 deals categorized: 4 STALLED (Brightpath, Helios, Zenith, Cruxfield) and 4 AT RISK (Orion, Solstice, Cobalt, Cascade). Each includes days since last activity vs. thresholds, days in stage expressed as multiples: Brightpath 3.5x, Helios 2.6x, Zenith 3.1x, Cruxfield 2.2x, Orion 2.2x, Solstice 1.2x, Cobalt 1.4x, Cascade 2.1x. Close dates flagged where passed (Brightpath, Orion). Reference table of average stage durations provided.

expectations.text: Brightpath Education ($410K) is flagged with compounding risk factors: close date 2026-03-15 has already passed, 42 days in Proposal Sent is 3.5x the 12-day average stage duration, no next step logged, and contact went dark — the recommended action addresses whether to attempt a final break-up email to force a response or move to close-lost with a logged loss reason, because $410K sitting in 'Proposal Sent' for 42 days with a passed close date is not pipeline, it is fiction that inflates the forecast
expectations.evidence: Brightpath flagged with 4 compounding risk factors: 42 days since activity (exceeds 30-day by 40%), 3.5x average stage duration, close date 4 days away with zero engagement, no next step. Assessment: 'This is $410,000 of pipeline that looks alive in the CRM but is almost certainly dead.' Action: send break-up email (specific language provided), 'If no response by Friday 2026-03-14, move to Close Lost with loss reason: Champion unresponsive -- no engagement for 6 weeks after proposal delivery.'

expectations.text: Orion Fleet Analytics ($310K) is flagged for both close date passed (2026-02-28, 11 days overdue) and 20 days without activity (exceeding the 14-day threshold), and the specific action addresses the unanswered 15% discount request — the rep has left money on the table by not responding with a counter-offer, and the action plan specifies that Sarah Chen should present a structured counter (e.g., 'offer 8% with annual commit, deadline Friday') rather than just 'respond to discount request'
expectations.evidence: Orion flagged with 4 risk factors including 'Expected close date 2026-02-28 has passed with no stage change -- 11 days overdue' and '20 days since last activity'. The discount request is identified as the most damaging factor. Action: 'Call the Orion contact today. Not an email -- a phone call.' Counter-offer: 'Offer 8% discount with an annual commitment and a signature deadline of Friday 2026-03-14.' Specific framing language provided.

expectations.text: Cascade Media Corp ($145K) is flagged for champion departure as a distinct risk criterion separate from the 29-day activity gap — the recommended action addresses the immediate need to identify a new champion or power sponsor at the account before any other outreach, because continuing to work a deal where your internal advocate has left the company without finding a replacement is the fastest path to a silent loss
expectations.evidence: Cascade lists 4 risk factors with champion departure as a distinct criterion (#4): 'Champion has left the company (identified via LinkedIn 2 weeks ago) and no new contact has been identified.' Assessment specifically addresses why this is different: 'When a champion leaves, three things happen simultaneously: (a) institutional knowledge about the evaluation is lost, (b) the new person may have different priorities, (c) internal momentum evaporates.' Action: identify replacement on LinkedIn first, then outreach referencing existing relationship.

expectations.text: Zenith Warehouse Automation ($88K, Discovery, 55 days stalled with no next step) or Brightpath ($410K with passed close date and dark contact) includes an explicit recommendation to close-lost or move to nurture, with the requirement to log a loss reason before removal — this enforces the NEVER rule that deals must not be removed without a loss reason, because loss-reason data is what prevents the team from repeating the same qualification mistakes next quarter
expectations.evidence: Zenith: 'STALLED / RECOMMEND CLOSE LOST OR NURTURE. If no response by 2026-03-18, move to Close Lost or Nurture (not active pipeline) with loss reason: "No engagement after initial demo -- prospect did not advance past Discovery." Log the loss reason.' Brightpath: 'If no response by Friday 2026-03-14, move to Close Lost with loss reason: "Champion unresponsive -- no engagement for 6 weeks after proposal delivery." Log the loss reason before removing.' Both explicitly require loss reason logging.

expectations.text: Each of the 8 deals has a concrete action plan that names the responsible rep, specifies the exact action to take (not 'follow up' but a particular move like 'send a break-up email referencing the January proposal,' 'call the partner who made the referral to re-engage the champion,' or 'present a counter-offer at 8% discount with a 48-hour expiration'), and sets a deadline within the current week — vague actions are operationally useless because reps will deprioritize them in favor of deals where they know what to do
expectations.evidence: All 8 deals have named rep + specific action + deadline: (1) Brightpath: Jake Torres, break-up email with specific language, Wed 2026-03-12. (2) Helios: Dana Okafor, LinkedIn message + multi-thread, Fri 2026-03-13. (3) Zenith: Dana Okafor, re-engagement email with specific language, Wed 2026-03-12. (4) Cruxfield: Dana Okafor, re-stage to Discovery + targeted email with two specific questions, Thu 2026-03-13. (5) Orion: Sarah Chen, phone call + 8% counter with Friday deadline, Tue 2026-03-11. (6) Solstice: Jake Torres, email to reframe legal + request legal intro, Wed 2026-03-12. (7) Cobalt: Sarah Chen, email + technical bridge offer, Thu 2026-03-13. (8) Cascade: Dana Okafor, identify replacement champion + targeted outreach, Wed 2026-03-12.

expectations.text: The total dollar value at risk across all 8 deals is quantified (approximately $2.22M) and contextualized against quota or pipeline — this gives the sales leader the answer to the only question that matters in the Monday meeting: 'how much of our pipeline is real and how much is at risk of evaporating,' which determines whether the team needs an emergency pipeline generation sprint or targeted deal rescue
expectations.evidence: Executive summary: 'Total pipeline under review: $2,218,000 across 8 deals.' Summary table shows STALLED: 4 deals/$1,538,000 and AT RISK: 4 deals/$815,000. States: '$2,218,000 in pipeline is at risk of evaporating. This is the number that answers the Monday meeting question: "How much of our pipeline is real?"' Includes per-rep exposure breakdown and recommended removals totaling $643K.
